skill.name: api-doc-generator
skill.description: Generate API documentation from code, including OpenAPI 3.0 outlines, request examples, and Postman collection guidance.
skill.summary: Create API documentation, OpenAPI drafts, examples, and Postman-ready request details from code.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:4 and high entropy at SKILL.md:1. Both are false positives: the cited lines are plain skill metadata and a YAML front matter marker, with no scripts, commands, network calls, encoded payloads, or prompt injection text found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The cited line is a Chinese description of API documentation generation. No cryptographic algorithm, security primitive, or executable code is present at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only front matter description text. There is no code path or algorithm reference that could create a cryptographic weakness.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: The cited line is the YAML front matter delimiter. The file content is readable metadata and short usage text, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain text across the skill file. The entropy alert is likely caused by multilingual text and symbols, not obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Manual API documentation falls behind code and creates confusion for developers. This skill helps produce OpenAPI drafts, request examples, and Postman collection guidance from available code or endpoint details.
actual_capabilities: Drafts API documentation from code snippets or endpoint descriptions.
Creates OpenAPI 3.0 style structures for routes, methods, parameters, and responses.
Produces request and response examples for developer reference pages.
Helps organize endpoint documentation for teams and small businesses.
Prepares guidance that can be adapted into Postman collections.
limitations: Requires human review before publishing generated API documentation.
Cannot guarantee complete coverage if source code or endpoint details are incomplete.
May need extra context for authentication, rate limits, and error models.
Does not execute code or verify live API behavior by itself.

use_cases.title: Document a New Service
use_cases.description: Generate a first draft of API reference content from route handlers and schema notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Integration Guides
use_cases.description: Turn endpoint details into clear request examples for external partners.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team API Specs
use_cases.description: Convert scattered endpoint notes into a consistent OpenAPI-oriented structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Endpoint Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate API documentation for these endpoints. Include method, path, purpose, parameters, request examples, and response examples.

prompt_templates.title: Build an OpenAPI Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this API code and create an OpenAPI 3.0 outline with paths, methods, request bodies, response schemas, and common errors.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Postman Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this endpoint documentation into Postman collection guidance. Include folders, request names, headers, variables, and example bodies.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these routes against the current API documentation. Identify missing endpoints, unclear schemas, inconsistent examples, and outdated response descriptions.

output_examples.input: A user service has create user, get user, update user, and delete user endpoints.
output_examples.output: A structured API reference with endpoint summaries, request fields, response examples, status codes, and OpenAPI-ready sections.

output_examples.input: A team has route code but no partner-facing integration documentation.
output_examples.output: A clear integration draft that explains authentication assumptions, request examples, expected responses, and Postman collection organization.

output_examples.input: An existing API spec may be missing recently added routes.
output_examples.output: A coverage review listing missing endpoints, incomplete schemas, unclear examples, and recommended documentation updates.

best_practices: Provide route code, schema definitions, and authentication details together.
Ask for examples that match real request and response shapes.
Review generated OpenAPI content before sharing it with customers.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated specifications without validating them against the implementation.
Do not omit authentication, error handling, or rate limit context.
Do not treat placeholder examples as production contract details.

faq.question: Can this skill create an OpenAPI 3.0 document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft OpenAPI-style sections from code or endpoint descriptions, but a developer should validate the result.

faq.question: Can it create Postman collections directly?
faq.answer: It can prepare Postman collection guidance and request organization. Import-ready output may need manual formatting.

faq.question: Does it inspect a live API?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes documentation work and does not test live endpoints by itself.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Route handlers, method and path lists, schema definitions, sample payloads, and authentication notes work best.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-English teams?
faq.answer: Yes. The source description is Chinese, and users can request documentation in their preferred language.

faq.question: Is the generated documentation final?
faq.answer: No. Generated documentation should be reviewed for accuracy, security details, and version-specific behavior.
